match.title: SRH vs LSG, IPL 2026 — 10th Match
match.result: LSG won by 5 wickets (with 1 ball remaining)

humdinger.narrative: A match of two halves and one remarkable rescue. SRH's collapse to 26/4 made this look a foregone conclusion, but Klaasen and Reddy's 116-run 5th-wicket partnership created real tension. The chase was controlled until a nervy final over — Pant's boundary with one ball remaining sealing it.

matchups.narrative: The defining exploitation. Reddy went after Rathi from over 11 with a ferocity that reframed the innings. Every over Rathi bowled at Reddy felt like a transaction with a predetermined winner.
Klaasen dominated Avesh in the middle but was finally snared at 18.1 — a classic case of a bowler staying patient and collecting the wicket when the batsman overcommitted to a premeditated shot.

Pant identified Unadkat as the bowling's weak link and milked him accordingly. The winning boundary in the final over came off Unadkat's delivery — a full ball outside off that Pant slapped over mid-off.
The mismatch in reverse. Shami's slower-ball mastery reduced Livingstone — ordinarily a destroyer of T20 attacks — to a passenger. Eight dots from 12 balls. That's not containment; that's domination.

turning_points.pressure_shift: SRH in immediate crisis. A slower ball outside off. Abhishek drives, edges, Siddharth dives at short third and takes it inches off the ground.
The single biggest momentum shift: from hopeless to competitive. The partnership would yield 116 off 56 balls.

turning_points.description: LSG need 13 from last over with Pant and Choudhary — 5 wickets down
turning_points.pressure_shift: From comfortable coasting to genuine nerves. SRH had a sniff. Pant's 50-ball 68* needed to reach the boundary one more time.

inflection_point.description: Unadkat to Pant, FOUR runs — LSG win with 1 ball remaining
inflection_point.narrative: Third time's the charm. Wide, full ball, Pant on one knee, bat nearly horizontal, slaps it over mid-off. A boundary that felt like it had been building for 50 balls.

black_mirror.title: What if Rathi had bowled to his IPL average?
black_mirror.outcome: SRH likely finish 144-148. With a target of 145, LSG's powerplay 53 already has them halfway there with 9 wickets in hand. Margin of victory: 6-7 wickets instead of 5. Klaasen-Reddy partnership still remarkable but context shifts dramatically.
black_mirror.probability_shift: LSG win probability at fall of 4th wicket: 72% vs original 58%

black_mirror.title: What if Shami hadn't been used as impact sub?
black_mirror.variable: Shami stays for LSG's batting innings (doesn't come on as impact sub)
black_mirror.outcome: Without Shami's 18 dots and 2 wickets in 4 overs, SRH likely post 175+. Now LSG need 176 — a completely different game. Markram's 45 off 27 gets them to 53/1 at the powerplay, but 120+ off the remaining 84 balls is another story entirely.
black_mirror.probability_shift: LSG win probability drops from 72% to approximately 40%

by_the_numbers.ratio_context: 3rd highest first:last 10-over ratio in T20 history (teams 130+)
by_the_numbers.note_pant: Pant's 68* had 9 fours, 0 sixes — unusual for a player known for aerial hitting

game_changers.reasoning: Set the match on its axis in overs 1-4. SRH were 26/4 after 7.1 overs; without Shami's devastating slower-ball spell, they likely reach 40-45/2 instead. The difference between a competitive 170 and a capped 156.
The innings that made 156 respectable from 26/4. Five sixes against four different bowlers. His 116-run 5th-wicket stand with Klaasen is what gave SRH's bowling attack something to defend.

The methodical anchor where you'd normally expect the adventurer. Pant played 50 balls — his longest T20 innings of the season — and hit 0 sixes. Every boundary was timed, not muscled. The winning shot was a one-knee slap, not a slog sweep.

Klaasen's 50 was more controlled than Reddy's but equally crucial. He rotated strike intelligently during the partnership, keeping Reddy on strike against weaker bowlers, then accelerating himself in the 14th-17th overs.

commentary_gems: 'Shami sets up LSG victory before Pant fifty takes them home' — the article that wrote itself
SRH vs LSG, Hyderabad 2026: 3rd highest first:last 10-over run ratio in T20 history (35 & 121)
Pant's 68* had 9 fours, 0 sixes. The most methodical innings of his IPL career?
'Swing and a miss, Pant throws down the striker's stumps. Harshal well short' — the last wicket, ball 123
'Third time's the charm and Pant wins it for LSG! Gentle tap of the glove to the partner'

srh_middle.note: SRH at 31/4 after 9, Klaasen-Reddy rebuild begins

srh_death.note: 116-run partnership broken at 18.1; SRH add 14 more with tail

lsg_powerplay.note: Best powerplay of the season for LSG at that point

lsg_middle.note: Three quick wickets 9.2-13.1; Pant holds firm

lsg_death.note: Pant needs 21 from last 2.5 overs; delivers
